title: Civil War Discussion #3 (HST 202)
html:
Yahir's hst 202 discussion essay on why the Union won and the Confederacy collapsed in the american civil war — citing industrial output, abraham lincoln's political adaptability, and enslaved-people resistance against the South's agrarian/states-rights structural weaknesses.

TL;DR

  • Argues Union victory came from 90% of US manufacturing capacity + 22M-vs-9M population edge + abraham lincoln's framing of war aims.
  • Cites david blight's "War So Terrible" lecture on adaptive emancipation as a war strategy that drained Southern labor.
  • Confederacy's collapse blamed on agrarian dependence on slavery, states-rights decentralization, and class divisions among Southern whites.
  • Key sources: american yawp Ch. 14, david blight lecture, notevenpast.org.
  • Part of the broader hst 202 coursework cluster (Reconstruction, Reconstruction primary-source analyses, Chinese exclusion).

hst 202 · american civil war · abraham lincoln · david blight · american yawp · slavery
